summary: Excellent soundboard capture of an all‑time great ’76 second set – ripping Help‑Slip‑Franklin’s, stellar Scarlet Begonias and blistering Franklin’s Tower.
review: The recording is a clean, crisp soundboard source that lets every instrument shine, while the performance itself is consistently praised as one of the most exciting shows of 1976. Reviewers rave about the explosive second set – especially the Help‑Slip‑Franklin’s sequence, a soaring Franklin’s Tower, and a jam‑filled Scarlet Begonias – and note Jerry Garcia’s focused, fire‑brand guitar work throughout.

ai_rating.rationale: Over two dozen reviews overwhelmingly rate the show 4–5 stars, repeatedly calling it a top‑tier ’76 performance with an outstanding second set and a clear, well‑mixed soundboard recording.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, balanced mix; reviewers note that every note can be heard clearly with no noticeable dropouts or muddiness.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: The second set builds from a tight Not Fade Away intro into a soaring St. Stephen, then erupts into the legendary Help‑Slip‑Franklin’s sequence and closes with high‑energy jams like Franklin's Tower; reviewers describe it as a seamless, exhilarating ride.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for fire‑brand, focused guitar work – screaming leads, intense chording and the first use of his Fender Twin Reverb on this run.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for “pounding out” the signature opening bass line on Help with authority.
band_member_comments.Drums: A brief banter before St. Stephen mentions "Uncle Bill"; overall drumming described as solid and driving.
